content.problemStatement:
Dragonado rarely gets stuck on a problem, unless of course, if it's a XOR Problem.

Dragonado while coming back from the Fruit Market, finds an array AAA of NNN elements. He wants to find 444 integers, say i1,i2,i3i_1,i_2,i_3i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč and i4i_4i4‚Äč, such that:


popcount(Ai1‚äēAi2)=popcount(Ai3‚äēAi4)\texttt{popcount}(A_{i_1} \oplus A_{i_2}) = \texttt{popcount}(A_{i_3} \oplus A_{i_4})popcount(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč‚äēAi2‚Äč‚Äč)=popcount(Ai3‚Äč‚Äč‚äēAi4‚Äč‚Äč)
1‚Č§i1,i2,i3,i4‚Č§N1 \le i_1,i_2,i_3,i_4 \le N1‚Č§i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč,i4‚Äč‚Č§N and  i1,i2,i3,i4i_1,i_2,i_3,i_4i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč,i4‚Äč are pairwise distinct.
Here, popcount(x)\texttt{popcount}(x)popcount(x) denotes the number of set bits in the binary representation of xxx. Secondly, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Can you help Dragonado find four integers i1,i2,i3i_1, i_2, i_3i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč and i4i_4i4‚Äč which satisfy the given conditions?

Output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if no answer exists.

If multiple answers exist, output **any**.

Note: The input of this problem is large, so use fast input/output methods.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output i1,i2,i3,i4i_1,i_2,i_3,i_4i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč,i4‚Äč which satisfy the given conditions.


Output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if no numbers satisfying the conditions exist.
constraints: It is guaranteed that sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: 1,4,2,31, 4, 2, 31,4,2,3 is a valid answer because:

popcount(A1‚äēA4)=popcount(7)=3\texttt{popcount}(A_{1} \oplus A_{4}) = \texttt{popcount}(7) = 3popcount(A1‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč)=popcount(7)=3
popcount(A2‚äēA3)=popcount(7)=3\texttt{popcount}(A_{2} \oplus A_{3}) = \texttt{popcount}(7) = 3popcount(A2‚Äč‚äēA3‚Äč)=popcount(7)=3

Therefore, popcount(Ai1‚äēAi2)=popcount(Ai3‚äēAi4)\texttt{popcount}(A_{i_1} \oplus A_{i_2}) = \texttt{popcount}(A_{i_3} \oplus A_{i_4})popcount(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč‚äēAi2‚Äč‚Äč)=popcount(Ai3‚Äč‚Äč‚äēAi4‚Äč‚Äč)
Test case-2: We can prove that no i1,i2,i3,i4i_1, i_2, i_3, i_4i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč,i4‚Äč exist that satisfy the given conditions.
